title: Americans are so enamored of equality
grift: Americans are so enamored of equality
body:
## "Americans are so enamored of equality that they would rather be equal in slavery than unequal in freedom."

Alexis de Tocqueville

title: Democracy extends the sphere of individual freedom
grift: Democracy extends the sphere of individual freedom
body:
## "Democracy extends the sphere of individual freedom, socialism restricts it. Democracy attaches all possible value to each man; socialism makes each man a mere agent, a mere number."

Alexis de Tocqueville

title: Despotism may govern without faith
grift: Despotism may govern without faith
body:
## "Despotism may govern without faith, but liberty cannot."

Alexis de Tocqueville

title: In democratic societies
grift: In democratic societies
body:
## "In democratic societies, each citizen is habitually engaged in the contemplation of a very puny object, namely, himself."

Alexis de Tocqueville

title: Liberty cannot be established without morality
grift: Liberty cannot be established without morality
body:
## "Liberty cannot be established without morality, nor morality without faith."

Alexis de Tocqueville

title: Society will develop a new kind of servitude
grift: Society will develop a new kind of servitude
body:
## "Society will develop a new kind of servitude which covers the surface of society with a network of complicated rules, through which the most original minds and the most energetic characters cannot penetrate."

Alexis de Tocqueville

title: The American Republic will endure
grift: The American Republic will endure
body:
## "The American Republic will endure until the day Congress discovers that it can bribe the public with the public's money."

Alexis de Tocqueville

title: The greatness of America
grift: The greatness of America
body:
## "The greatness of America lies not in being more enlightened than any other nation, but rather in her ability to repair her faults."

Alexis de Tocqueville

title: The health of a democratic society
grift: The health of a democratic society
body:
## "The health of a democratic society may be measured by the quality of functions performed by private citizens."

Alexis de Tocqueville

title: There are many men of principle
grift: There are many men of principle
body:
## "There are many men of principle in both parties in America, but there is no party of principle."

Alexis de Tocqueville

title: A society that does not recognize
grift: A society that does not recognize
body:
## "A society that does not recognize that each individual has values of his own which he is entitled to follow can have no respect for the dignity of the individual and cannot really know freedom."

F.A. Hayek

title: Freedom is the will to be responsible to ourselves
grift: Freedom is the will to be responsible to ourselves
body:
## "Freedom is the will to be responsible to ourselves."

Friedrich Hayek

title: Hayek: The Fatal Conceit
grift: Hayek: The Fatal Conceit
body:
## "The Fatal Conceit: The curious task of economics is to demonstrate to men how little they really know about what they imagine they can design"

Friedrich Hayek

title: The freedom of the individual
grift: The freedom of the individual
body:
## "The freedom of the individual is the ultimate goal in a free society."

Friedrich Hayek

title: The more the state plans
grift: The more the state plans
body:
## "The more the state plans, the more difficult planning becomes for the individual."

Friedrich Hayek

title: The road to serfdom is paved with good intentions
grift: The road to serfdom is paved with good intentions
body:
## "The road to serfdom is paved with good intentions."

Friedrich Hayek

title: There is all the difference in the world
grift: There is all the difference in the world
body:
## "There is all the difference in the world between treating people equally and attempting to make them equal."

Friedrich Hayek

title: To be controlled in our economic pursuits
grift: To be controlled in our economic pursuits
body:
## "To be controlled in our economic pursuits means to be controlled in everything."

Friedrich Hayek

title: Liberty is the right to tell people what they do not want to hear
grift: Liberty is the right to tell people what they do not want to hear
body:
## "Liberty is the right to tell people what they do not want to hear."

George Orwell

title: Freedom is the right to tell people what they do not want to hear
grift: Freedom is the right to tell people what they do not want to hear
body:
## "Freedom is the right to tell people what they do not want to hear."

George Orwell

title: If liberty means anything at all
grift: If liberty means anything at all
body:
## "If liberty means anything at all, it means the right to tell people what they do not want to hear."

George Orwell

title: The real division is not between conservatives and revolutionaries
grift: The real division is not between conservatives and revolutionaries
body:
## "The real division is not between conservatives and revolutionaries but between authoritarians and libertarians."

George Orwell

title: Who controls the past controls the future
grift: Who controls the past controls the future
body:
## "Who controls the past controls the future. Who controls the present controls the past."

George Orwell

title: Economics is not about goods and services
grift: Economics is not about goods and services
body:
## "Economics is not about goods and services; it is about human choice and action."

Ludwig von Mises

title: Economics is not about goods and services
grift: Economics is not about goods and services
body:
## "Economics is not about goods and services; it is about human choice and action."

Ludwig Von Mises

title: Government cannot make man richer
grift: Government cannot make man richer
body:
## "Government cannot make man richer, but it can make him poorer."

Ludwig von Mises

title: Liberalism is not anarchism
grift: Liberalism is not anarchism
body:
## "Liberalism is not anarchism, nor has it anything whatsoever to do with anarchism. The liberal understands quite clearly that without resort to compulsion, the existence of society would be endangered and that behind the rules of conduct whose observance is necessary to assure peaceful human cooperation must stand the threat of force if the whole edifice of society is not to be continually at the mercy of any one of its members. One must be in a position to compel the person who will not respect the lives, health, personal freedom, or private property of others to acquiesce in the rules of life in society. This is the function that the liberal doctrine assigns to the state: the protection of property, liberty, and peace."

Ludwig von Mises

title: Repression by brute force is always
grift: Repression by brute force is always
body:
## "Repression by brute force is always a confession of the inability to make use of the better weapons of the intellect—better because they alone give promise of final success."

Ludwig von Mises

title: The issue is always the same: the government or
grift: The issue is always the same: the government or
body:
## "The issue is always the same: the government or the market. There is no third solution."

Ludwig von Mises

title: The problem is not to find better people
grift: The problem is not to find better people
body:
## "The problem is not to find better people to govern, but to allow a system that lets the governed govern themselves."

Ludwig von Mises

title: The program of classical liberalism = property
grift: The program of classical liberalism = property
body:
## "The program of classical liberalism, condensed into a single word, would have to read: property."

Ludwig von Mises

title: The worst evils which mankind has ever had to endure
grift: The worst evils which mankind has ever had to endure
body:
## "The worst evils which mankind has ever had to endure were inflicted by bad governments."

Ludwig von Mises

title: Whoever wishes to be free must
grift: Whoever wishes to be free must
body:
## "Whoever wishes to be free must make himself the master of his own passions."

Ludwig von Mises

title: A man's right to work
grift: A man's right to work
body:
## "A man's right to work as he will, to spend what he earns, to own property, to have the state as servant and not as master, they are essence of a tree economy, and on that freedom all our other freedoms depend."

Margaret Thatcher

title: Being powerful is like being a lady
grift: Being powerful is like being a lady
body:
## "Being powerful is like being a lady. If you have to tell people you are, you aren't."

Margaret Thatcher

title: Disciplining yourself to do what you know is right
grift: Disciplining yourself to do what you know is right
body:
## "Disciplining yourself to do what you know is right and important, although difficult, is the highroad to pride, self-esteem, and personal satisfaction."

Margaret Thatcher

title: Europe will never be like America
grift: Europe will never be like America
body:
## "Europe will never be like America. Europe is a product of history. America is a product of philosophy."

Margaret Thatcher

title: Free enterprise has enabled
grift: Free enterprise has enabled
body:
## "Free enterprise has enabled the creative and the acquisitive urges of man to be given expression in a way which benefits all members of society."

Margaret Thatcher

title: If they attack you personally
grift: If they attack you personally
body:
## "If they attack you personally it means they have not a single political argument left"

Margaret Thatcher

title: Let us never forget this fundamental truth
grift: Let us never forget this fundamental truth
body:
## "Let us never forget this fundamental truth: If the State wishes to spend more it can do so only by borrowing your savings or by taxing you more. It is no good thinking that someone else will pay - that 'someone else' is you."

Margaret Thatcher

title: Whenever we can, let us, like Luther, nail the truth to the door and let us do it in unambiguous English.
grift: Whenever we can, let us, like Luther, nail the truth to the door and let us do it in unambiguous English.
body:
## "Whenever we can, let us, like Luther, nail the truth to the door and let us do it in unambiguous English."

Margaret Thatcher

title: One way to destroy capitalism
grift: One way to destroy capitalism
body:
## "One way to destroy capitalism, said Lenin, was to devalue its currency. Another way is to debase its language."

Margaret Thatcher

title: Perhaps people do want more choice, do want to own their own homes, do want a greater say in their children's education.
grift: Perhaps people do want more choice, do want to own their own homes, do want a greater say in their children's education.
body:
## "Perhaps people do want more choice, do want to own their own homes, do want a greater say in their children's education."

Margaret Thatcher

title: Perhaps the pursuit of equality at any price does undermine liberty.
grift: Perhaps the pursuit of equality at any price does undermine liberty.
body:
## "Perhaps the pursuit of equality at any price does undermine liberty."

Margaret Thatcher

title: Socialism is like one of those horrible viruses
grift: Socialism is like one of those horrible viruses
body:
## "Socialism is like one of those horrible viruses: you no sooner discover a remedy for one version, than it spontaneously evolves into another."

Margaret Thatcher

title: Socialists say 'equality.' What they mean is 'levelling down'
grift: Socialists say 'equality.' What they mean is 'levelling down'
body:
## "Socialists say 'equality.' What they mean is 'levelling down'"

Margaret Thatcher

title: Socialists say 'Government aid'. What they mean is 'taxpayers' aid.
grift: Socialists say 'Government aid'. What they mean is 'taxpayers' aid.
body:
## "Socialists say 'Government aid'. What they mean is 'taxpayers' aid."

Margaret Thatcher

title: Socialists say 'Publicly owned'. What they mean is 'State controlled'
grift: Socialists say 'Publicly owned'. What they mean is 'State controlled'
body:
## "Socialists say 'Publicly owned'. What they mean is 'State controlled'"

Margaret Thatcher

title: Socialists say 'social justice'. What they mean is 'selective justice'.
grift: Socialists say 'social justice'. What they mean is 'selective justice'.
body:
## "Socialists say 'social justice'. What they mean is 'selective justice'."

Margaret Thatcher

title: Some socialists seem to believe
grift: Some socialists seem to believe
body:
## "Some socialists seem to believe that people should be numbers in a State computer. We believe they should be individuals. We are all unequal. No one, thank heavens, is like anyone else, however much the socialists may pretend otherwise. We believe that everyone has the right to be unequal but to us every human being is equally important."

Margaret Thatcher

title: Standing in the middle of the road is very dangerous
grift: Standing in the middle of the road is very dangerous
body:
## "Standing in the middle of the road is very dangerous; you get knocked down by traffic from both sides."

Margaret Thatcher

title: The best reply to full-blooded Socialism
grift: The best reply to full-blooded Socialism
body:
## "The best reply to full-blooded Socialism is not milk and water Socialism, it is genuine Conservatism."

Margaret Thatcher

title: The Left would rather the poor were poorer
grift: The Left would rather the poor were poorer
body:
## "The Left would rather the poor were poorer, provided the rich were less rich."

Margaret Thatcher

title: The problem with socialism
grift: The problem with socialism
body:
## "The problem with socialism is that you eventually run out of other people's money."

Margaret Thatcher

title: The rule of law is the most civilized concept
grift: The rule of law is the most civilized concept
body:
## "The rule of law is the most civilized concept humanity has yet devised."

Margaret Thatcher

title: The single biggest error during
grift: The single biggest error during
body:
## “The single biggest error during my political lifetime has been to confuse freedom with equality”

Margaret Thatcher

title: The Socialist myth
grift: The Socialist myth
body:
## "The Socialist myth is that governments can promise more and more, and take more and more, but that we can still keep our freedom. The truth is that if government does everything for you, it will take everything from you — first your money, then your dignity, and finally your freedom."

Margaret Thatcher

title: The state is not equipped to run businesses
grift: The state is not equipped to run businesses
body:
## "The state is not equipped to run businesses. It is equipped to run the law."

Margaret Thatcher

title: The trouble with socialism
grift: The trouble with socialism
body:
## "The trouble with socialism is that it always ends up running out of other people’s money."

Margaret Thatcher

title: The two totalitarian systems
grift: The two totalitarian systems
body:
## "The two totalitarian systems which we in Britain had to fight — Nazism and Communism — two socialist tyrannies, let's remember - represented one single model.

## The other was represented by our Anglo-American liberal political culture — our parliamentary institutions, our law, our notions of human rights and our free enterprise system based on private property."

Margaret Thatcher

title: Wealth is not created by government
grift: Wealth is not created by government
body:
## "Wealth is not created by government; it is created by the talent and enterprise of individuals in an atmosphere of freedom."

Margaret Thatcher

title: You can't make people good
grift: You can't make people good
body:
## "You can't make people good, kind, generous, thoughtful, or dutiful by compulsion"

Margaret Thatcher

title: If a private enterprise is a failure, it closes down
grift: If a private enterprise is a failure, it closes down
body:
## "If a private enterprise is a failure, it closes down - unless it can get a government subsidy to keep it going; if a government enterprise fails, it is expanded. I challenge you to find exceptions."

Milton Friedman

title: If you want to help the poor, prove it by getting rich yourself
grift: If you want to help the poor, prove it by getting rich yourself
body:
## "If you want to help the poor, demonstrate to them that they can become rich; prove it by getting rich yourself."

Milton Friedman

title: A society that puts equality before freedom
grift: A society that puts equality before freedom
body:
## "A society that puts equality before freedom will get neither. A society that puts freedom before equality will get a high degree of both."

Milton Friedman

title: After the fall of communism everybody
grift: After the fall of communism everybody
body:
## "After the fall of communism, everybody in the world agreed that Socialism was a failure. Everybody in the world, more or less, agreed that Capitalism was a success. And every Capitalist country in the world apparently deduced from that what the West needed was more Socialism."

Milton Friedman

title: Concentrated power is not rendered harmless
grift: Concentrated power is not rendered harmless
body:
## "Concentrated power is not rendered harmless by the good intentions of those who create it."

Milton Friedman

title: If you put the federal government in charge of the Sahara Desert
grift: If you put the federal government in charge of the Sahara Desert
body:
## "If you put the federal government in charge of the Sahara Desert, in five years there'd be a shortage of sand."

Milton Friedman

title: Inflation is taxation without legislation
grift: Inflation is taxation without legislation
body:
## "Inflation is taxation without legislation."

Milton Friedman

title: Nothing is more permanent than a temporary government measure
grift: Nothing is more permanent than a temporary government measure
body:
## "Nothing is more permanent than a temporary government measure."

Milton Friedman

title: Nothing is so permanent
grift: Nothing is so permanent
body:
## "Nothing is so permanent as a temporary government program."

Milton Friedman

title: The freedom to fail is vital
grift: The freedom to fail is vital
body:
## "The freedom to fail is vital if we are to have the freedom to succeed."

Milton Friedman

title: The government solution to a problem is usually as bad as the problem
grift: The government solution to a problem is usually as bad as the problem
body:
## "The government solution to a problem is usually as bad as the problem."

Milton Friedman

title: There is no such thing as a free lunch
grift: There is no such thing as a free lunch
body:
## "There is no such thing as a free lunch."

Milton Friedman

title: Underlying most arguments against the free market
grift: Underlying most arguments against the free market
body:
## "Underlying most arguments against the free market is a lack of belief in freedom itself."

Milton Friedman

title: All great change in America begins at the dinner table
grift: All great change in America begins at the dinner table
body:
## "All great change in America begins at the dinner table."

Ronald Reagan

title: As government expands, liberty contracts
grift: As government expands, liberty contracts
body:
## "As government expands, liberty contracts."

Ronald Reagan

title: Freedom is never more than one generation away from extinction
grift: Freedom is never more than one generation away from extinction
body:
## "Freedom is never more than one generation away from extinction."

Ronald Reagan

title: Government’s first duty is to protect the people
grift: Government’s first duty is to protect the people
body:
## "Government’s first duty is to protect the people, not run their lives."

Ronald Reagan

title: Government's first duty is to protect the people
grift: Government's first duty is to protect the people
body:
## "Government's first duty is to protect the people, is to protect the people, not run their lives."

Ronald Reagan

title: Government's view of the economy
grift: Government's view of the economy
body:
## "Government's view of the economy could be summed up in a few short phrases: If it moves, tax it. If it keeps moving, regulate it. And if it stops moving, subsidize it."

Ronald Reagan

title: History teaches that war begins
grift: History teaches that war begins
body:
## "History teaches that war begins when governments believe the price of aggression is cheap."

Ronald Reagan

title: Man is not free unless government is limited
grift: Man is not free unless government is limited
body:
## "Man is not free unless government is limited."

Ronald Reagan

title: No government ever voluntarily reduces itself in size
grift: No government ever voluntarily reduces itself in size
body:
## "No government ever voluntarily reduces itself in size. Government programs, once launched, never disappear."

Ronald Reagan

title: The future doesn’t belong to the fainthearted
grift: The future doesn’t belong to the fainthearted
body:
## "The future doesn’t belong to the fainthearted; it belongs to the brave."

Ronald Reagan

title: The nine most terrifying words in the English language are
grift: The nine most terrifying words in the English language are
body:
## "The nine most terrifying words in the English language are "I'm from the government, and I'm here to help."

Ronald Reagan

title: The problem is not that people are taxed too little
grift: The problem is not that people are taxed too little
body:
## "The problem is not that people are taxed too little, the problem is that government spends too much."

Ronald Reagan

title: The trouble with our liberal friends
grift: The trouble with our liberal friends
body:
## "The trouble with our liberal friends is not that they're ignorant it's just that they know so much that isn't so.'

Ronald Reagan

title: We are a nation that has a government
grift: We are a nation that has a government
body:
## "We are a nation that has a government - not the other way around."

Ronald Reagan

title: We must reject the idea
grift: We must reject the idea
body:
## "We must reject the idea that every time a law's broken, society is guilty rather than the lawreaker. It is time to restore the American precept that each individual is accountable for his actions."

Ronald Reagan

title: You can’t be for big government
grift: You can’t be for big government
body:
## "You can’t be for big government, big taxes, and big bureaucracy and still be for the little guy."

Ronald Reagan

title: Activism is a way for useless people to feel important
grift: Activism is a way for useless people to feel important
body:
## "Activism is a way for useless people to feel important, even if the consequences of their activism are counterproductive for those they claim to be helping and damaging to the fabric of society as a whole."

Thomas Sowell

title: Blacks were not enslaved because they were black
grift: Blacks were not enslaved because they were black
body:
## "Blacks were not enslaved because they were black but because they were available. Slavery has existed in the world for thousands of years. Whites enslaved other whites in Europe for centuries before the first black was brought to the Western hemisphere. Asians enslaved Europeans. Asians enslaved other Asians. Africans enslaved other Africans, and indeed even today in North Africa, blacks continue to enslave blacks."

Thomas Sowell

title: Both free speech rights and property rights
grift: Both free speech rights and property rights
body:
## "Both free speech rights and property rights belong legally to individuals, but their real function is social, to benefit vast numbers of people who do not themselves exercise these."

Thomas Sowell

title: Equality of rights does not mean
grift: Equality of rights does not mean
body:
## "Equality of rights does not mean equality of results. I can have all the equal treatment in the world on a golf course and I will not finish within shouting distance of Tiger Woods."

Thomas Sowell

title: Europe is belatedly discovering
grift: Europe is belatedly discovering
body:
## "Europe is belatedly discovering how unbelievably stupid it was to import millions of people from cultures that despise Western values and which often promote hatred toward the people who have let them in."

Thomas Sowell

title: Freedom is unlikely to be lost
grift: Freedom is unlikely to be lost
body:
## "Freedom is unlikely to be lost all at once and openly. It is far more likely to be eroded away, bit by bit, amid glittering promises and expressions of noble."

Thomas Sowell

title: Guilt has so furtively stolen into many hearts
grift: Guilt has so furtively stolen into many hearts
body:
## "Guilt has so furtively stolen into many hearts and minds that people feel apologetic about being civilized, educated, and productive when others are barbaric, uneducated, and parasitic. When civilization apologizes to barbarism, something has gone very wrong at a very fundamental level."

Thomas Sowell

title: Human beings are going to make mistakes
grift: Human beings are going to make mistakes
body:
## "Human beings are going to make mistakes, whether in the market or in the government. The difference is that survival in the market requires recognizing mistakes and changing course before you go bankrupt. But survival in politics requires denying mistakes and sticking with the policies you advocated, while blaming others for the bad results."

Thomas Sowell

title: I have never understood why it is 'greed'
grift: I have never understood why it is 'greed'
body:
## "I have never understood why it is 'greed' to want to keep the money you've earned, but not greed to want to take somebody else's money."

Thomas Sowell

title: If you are not prepared to use force
grift: If you are not prepared to use force
body:
## "If you are not prepared to use force to defend civilization, then be prepared to accept barbarism."

Thomas Sowell

title: If you cannot achieve equality
grift: If you cannot achieve equality
body:
## "If you cannot achieve equality of performance among people born to the same parents and raised under the same roof, how realistic is it to expect to achieve it across broader and deeper social divisions?"

Thomas Sowell

title: If you want more of something, subsidize it
grift: If you want more of something, subsidize it
body:
## "If you want more of something, subsidize it; if you want less of something, tax it."

Thomas Sowell

title: If you want to see the poor remain poor
grift: If you want to see the poor remain poor
body:
## "If you want to see the poor remain poor, generation after generation, just keep the standards low in their schools and make excuses for their academic shortcomings and personal misbehavior. But please don't congratulate yourself on your compassion."

Thomas Sowell

title: Immigration laws are the only laws
grift: Immigration laws are the only laws
body:
## "Immigration laws are the only laws that are discussed in terms of how to help people who break them."

Thomas Sowell

title: 'Income distribution'
grift: 'Income distribution'
body:
## "Despite a voluminous and often fervent literature on 'income distribution', the cold fact is that most income is not distributed: It is earned."

Thomas Sowell

title: It is hard to imagine a more stupid or more dangerous way of making decisions
grift: It is hard to imagine a more stupid or more dangerous way of making decisions
body:
## "It is hard to imagine a more stupid or more dangerous way of making decisions than by putting those decisions in the hands of people who pay no price for being wrong."

Thomas Sowell

title: It is usually futile to try to talk
grift: It is usually futile to try to talk
body:
## "It is usually futile to try to talk facts and analysis to people who are enjoying a sense of moral superiority in their ignorance."

Thomas Sowell

title: It was Thomas Edison who brought
grift: It was Thomas Edison who brought
body:
## "It was Thomas Edison who brought us electricity not the Sierra Club. It was the Wright Brothers who got us off the ground not the Federal Aviation Administration.

## It was Henry Ford who ended the isolation of millions of Americans by making the automobile affordable, not Ralph Nader.

## Those who have helped the poor the most have not been those who have gone around loudly expressing compassion for the poor but those who found ways to make industry more productive and distribution more efficient so that the poor of today can afford things that the affluent of yesterday could only dream about."

Thomas Sowell

title: It's amazing how fast people learn
grift: It's amazing how fast people learn
body:
## "It's amazing how fast people learn when they are not insulated from the consequences of their decisions."

Thomas Sowell

title: It is fascinating to watch politicians
grift: It is fascinating to watch politicians
body:
## "It is fascinating to watch politicians come up with 'solutions' to problems that are a direct result of their previous solutions. In many cases the most efficient thing to do would be to repeal their previous solution and stop being so gung-ho for creating new solutions."

Thomas Sowell

title: Liberalism is totalitarianism with a human face.
grift: Liberalism is totalitarianism with a human face.
body:
## "Liberalism is totalitarianism with a human face."

Thomas Sowell

title: Many on the political left
grift: Many on the political left
body:
## "Many on the political left are so entranced by the beauty of their vision that they cannot see the ugly reality they are creating in the real world."

Thomas Sowell

title: Morally and intellectually superior
grift: Morally and intellectually superior
body:
## "The real motives of liberals have nothing to do with the welfare of other people. Instead, they have two related goals — to establish themselves as morally and intellectually superior to the rather distasteful population of common people, and to gather as much power as possible to tell those distasteful common people how they must live their lives."

Thomas Sowell

title: More whites were brought as slaves
grift: More whites were brought as slaves
body:
## "More whites were brought as slaves to North Africa than blacks brought as slaves to the United States or to the 13 colonies from which it was formed. White slaves were still being bought and sold in the Ottoman Empire, decades after blacks were freed in the United States."

Thomas Sowell

title: Much of what are called 'social problems'
grift: Much of what are called 'social problems'
body:
## "Much of what are called 'social problems' consists of the fact that intellectuals have theories that do not fit the real world. From this they conclude that it is the real world which is wrong and needs changing."

Thomas Sowell

title: Multiculturalism, like the caste system
grift: Multiculturalism, like the caste system
body:
## "Multiculturalism, like the caste system, tends to freeze people where the accident of birth has placed them."

Thomas Sowell

title: No matter how disastrously some policy
grift: No matter how disastrously some policy
body:
## "No matter how disastrously some policy has turned out, anyone who criticizes it can expect to hear: 'But what would you replace it with?' When you put out a fire, what do you replace it with?"

Thomas Sowell

title: Nobody is equal to anybody
grift: Nobody is equal to anybody
body:
## "Nobody is equal to anybody. Even the same man is not equal to himself on different days."

Thomas Sowell

title: One of many disservices done to young people
grift: One of many disservices done to young people
body:
## "One of many disservices done to young people is giving them the puffed up notion that they are in a position to pass sweeping judgments on a world that they have barely begun to experience."

Thomas Sowell

title: One of the most important reasons
grift: One of the most important reasons
body:
## "One of the most important reasons for studying history is that virtually every stupid idea that is in vogue today has been tried before and proved disastrous before, time and again."

Thomas Sowell

title: Ours may become the first civilization destroyed
grift: Ours may become the first civilization destroyed
body:
## "Ours may become the first civilization destroyed, not by the power of our enemies, but by the ignorance of our teachers and the dangerous nonsense they are teaching our children."

Thomas Sowell

title: People who are very aware
grift: People who are very aware
body:
## "People who are very aware that they have more knowledge than the average person are often very unaware that they do not have one-tenth of the knowledge of all of the average persons put together. In this situation, for the intelligentsia to impose their notions on ordinary people is essentially to impose ignorance on knowledge."

Thomas Sowell

title: People who pride themselves
grift: People who pride themselves
body:
## "People who pride themselves on their 'complexity' and deride others for being 'simplistic' should realize that the truth is often not very complicated. What gets complex is evading the truth."

Thomas Sowell

title: Racism is not dead
grift: Racism is not dead
body:
## "Racism is not dead, but it is on life support — kept alive by politicians, race hustlers and people who get a sense of superiority by denouncing others as 'racists'."

Thomas Sowell

title: Since wealth is the only thing
grift: Since wealth is the only thing
body:
## "Since wealth is the only thing that can cure poverty, you might think that the left would be as obsessed with the creation of wealth as they are with the redistribution of wealth. But you would be wrong."

Thomas Sowell

**_That's because they don't care about eradicating poverty they want power._**

title: Socialism in general has a record of failure
grift: Socialism in general has a record of failure
body:
## "Socialism in general has a record of failure so blatant that only an intellectual could ignore or evade"

Thomas Sowell

title: Some Americans will never appreciate America
grift: Some Americans will never appreciate America
body:
## "Some Americans will never appreciate America, until after they have helped destroy it, and have then begun to suffer the consequences."

Thomas Sowell

title: The beauty of doing nothing
grift: The beauty of doing nothing
body:
## "The beauty of doing nothing is that you can do it perfectly. Only when you do something is it almost impossible to do it without mistakes. Therefore people who are contributing nothing to society, except their constant criticisms, can feel both intellectually and morally superior."

Thomas Sowell

title: The black family which survived slavery
grift: The black family which survived slavery
body:
## "The black family survived centuries of slavery and generations of segregation, but it has disintegrated in the wake of the Liberals' expansion of the welfare state."

Thomas Sowell

title: The fact that crime and poverty
grift: The fact that crime and poverty
body:
## "The fact that crime and poverty are correlated is automatically taken to mean that poverty causes crime, rather than that similar attitudes or behavior patterns may contribute to both poverty and crime."

Thomas Sowell

title: The first lesson of economics is scarcity
grift: The first lesson of economics is scarcity
body:
## "The first lesson of economics is scarcity: there is never enough of anything to fully satisfy all those who want it. The first lesson of politics is to disregard the first lesson of economics."

Thomas Sowell

title: The great danger to liberty
grift: The great danger to liberty
body:
## "The great danger to liberty is that the state will come to regard all liberty as a threat to itself."

Thomas Sowell

title: The left takes its vision seriously
grift: The left takes its vision seriously
body:
## "The left takes its vision seriously — more seriously than it takes the rights of other people. They want to be our shepherds, but that requires us to be sheep."

Thomas Sowell

title: The more I study the history of
grift: The more I study the history of
body:
## "The more I study the history of intellectuals, the more they seem like a wrecking crew, dismantling civilization bit by bit — replacing what works with what sounds good."

Thomas Sowell

title: The most basic question is
grift: The most basic question is
body:
## "The most basic question is not what is best, but who shall decide what is best."

Thomas Sowell

title: The next time some academics tell you
grift: The next time some academics tell you
body:
## "The next time some academics tell you how important diversity is, ask how many Republicans there are in their sociology department."

Thomas Sowell

title: The purpose of politics
grift: The purpose of politics
body:
## "The purpose of politics is not to solve problems but to find problems to justify the expansion of government power and an increase in taxes."

Thomas Sowell

title: The very same people who say
grift: The very same people who say
body:
## "The very same people who say that government has no right to interfere with sexual activity between consenting adults believe that the government has every right to interfere with economic activity between consenting adults."

Thomas Sowell

title: The welfare state is not really about the welfare of the masses
grift: The welfare state is not really about the welfare of the masses
body:
## "The welfare state is not really about the welfare of the masses. It is about the egos of the elites."

Thomas Sowell

title: The welfare state is the oldest con game
grift: The welfare state is the oldest con game
body:
## "The welfare state is the oldest con game in the world. First you take people’s money away quietly and then you give some of it back to them flamboyantly."

Thomas Sowell

title: There are no solutions, only trade-offs
grift: There are no solutions, only trade-offs
body:
## "There are no solutions, only trade-offs."

Thomas Sowell

title: There are no solutions
grift: There are no solutions
body:
## "There are no solutions. There are only trade-offs."

Thomas Sowell

title: There has now been created a world
grift: There has now been created a world
body:
## "There has now been created a world in which the success of others is a grievance, rather than an example."

Thomas Sowell

title: There have always been ignorant people
grift: There have always been ignorant people
body:
## "There have always been ignorant people but they haven't always had college degrees to make them unaware of their ignorance some people imagine that they are well informed because they have memorized a whole galaxy of trendy dogmas and fashionable attitudes."

Thomas Sowell

title: Those who cry out that the government should 'do something'
grift: Those who cry out that the government should 'do something'
body:
## "Those who cry out that the government should 'do something' never even ask what the government is qualified to do."

Thomas Sowell

title: Too much of what is called education
grift: Too much of what is called education
body:
## "Too much of what is called 'education' is little more than an expensive isolation from reality"

Thomas Sowell

title: We are a nation of immigrants
grift: We are a nation of immigrants
body:
## ""We are a nation of immigrants," we are constantly reminded. We are also a nation of people with ten fingers and ten toes. Does that mean that anyone who has ten fingers and ten toes should be welcomed and given American citizenship?"

Thomas Sowell

title: Welfare states on both sides
grift: Welfare states on both sides
body:
## "Welfare states on both sides of the Atlantic have discovered that largesse to losers does not reduce their hostility to society, but only increases it. Far from producing gratitude, generosity is seen as an admission of guilt, and the reparations as inadequate compensations for injustices - leading to worsening behavior by the recipients."

Thomas Sowell

title: What do you call it when someone steals money
grift: What do you call it when someone steals money
body:
## "What do you call it when someone steals money secretly? Theft. What do you call it when someone takes money by force? Robbery. What do you call it when a politician takes someone else's money and gives it to someone likely to vote for him? Social Justice."

Thomas Sowell

title: What exactly is your fair share
grift: What exactly is your fair share
body:
## "What exactly is your fair share of what someone else has worked for?"

Thomas Sowell

title: What 'multiculturalism' boils down to
grift: What 'multiculturalism' boils down to
body:
## "What 'multiculturalism' boils down to is that you can praise any culture in the world except Western culture-and you cannot blame any culture in the world except Western culture."

Thomas Sowell

title: When I was growing up
grift: When I was growing up
body:
## "When I was growing up, we were taught the stories of people whose inventions and scientific discoveries had expanded the lives of millions of other people. Today, students are being taught to admire those who complain, denounce and demand."

Thomas Sowell

title: When politicians say 'spread the wealth'
grift: When politicians say 'spread the wealth'
body:
## "When politicians say 'spread the wealth,' translate that as 'concentrate the power,' because that is the only way they can spread the wealth. And once they get the power concentrated, they can do anything else they want to, as people have discovered - often to their horror - in countries around the world."

Thomas Sowell

title: Where nobody is responsible for what they did
grift: Where nobody is responsible for what they did
body:
## "We seem to be getting closer and closer to a situation where nobody is responsible for what they did but we are all responsible for what somebody else did."

Thomas Sowell

title: You have to have a sense of humor
grift: You have to have a sense of humor
body:
## "You have to have a sense of humor if you follow politics. Otherwise, the sheer fraudulence of it all will get you down."

Thomas Sowell

title: It constantly amazes me
grift: It constantly amazes me
body:
## "It constantly amazes me that defenders of the free market are expected to offer certainty and perfection, while government has only to make promises and offer good intentions."

Lawrence Reed

title: Marxism has led to Fascism
grift: Marxism has led to Fascism
body:
## "Marxism has led to Fascism and National Socialism, because, in all essentials, it is Fascism and National Socialism."

Friedrich Hayek

title: Prior to capitalism
grift: Prior to capitalism
body:
## "Prior to capitalism, the way people amassed great wealth was by looting, plundering and enslaving their fellow man. Capitalism made it possible to become wealthy by serving your fellow man."

Walter E Williams

title: Real Socialism' has never been tried
grift: Real Socialism' has never been tried
body:
## "'Real Socialism' has never been tried in the same way that 'Real Capitalism' has never been tried. The difference is 'Almost Socialism' resulted in the impoverishment & death of hundreds of millions of people. While 'Almost Capitalism' has lifted billions from absolute poverty."

@Mark Antro

title: The act of reaching into one's own pockets
grift: The act of reaching into one's own pockets
body:
## "The act of reaching into one's own pockets to help a fellow man in need is praiseworthy and laudable. Reaching into someone else's pocket is despicable and worthy of condemnation."

Walter E Williams

title: The history of the 20th century is full
grift: The history of the 20th century is full
body:
## "The history of the 20th century is full of examples of countries that set out to redistribute wealth and ended up redistributing poverty."

Thomas Sowell

title: The Constitution is not a living organism
grift: The Constitution is not a living organism
body:
## "The Constitution is not a living organism. It's a legal document, and it says what it says and doesn't say what it doesn't say."

Antonin Scalia

title: The Constitution was made to guard the people
grift: The Constitution was made to guard the people
body:
## "The Constitution was made to guard the people against the dangers of good intentions."

Daniel Webster

title: To live under the American Constitution
grift: To live under the American Constitution
body:
## "To live under the American Constitution is the greatest political privilege that was ever accorded to the human race."

Calvin Coolidge

title: Can a people tax themselves into prosperity?
grift: Can a people tax themselves into prosperity?
body:
## "Can a people tax themselves into prosperity? Can a man stand in a bucket and lift himself up by the handle?"

Winston Churchill

title: Have you ever noticed how statists
grift: Have you ever noticed how statists
body:
## "Have you ever noticed how Statists are constantly 'reforming' their own handiwork education reform health care reform welfare reform tax reform the very fact they're always busy 'reforming' is an implicit admission that they didn't get it right the first 50 times"

Lawrence Reed

title: A liberal is someone who feels a great debt
grift: A liberal is someone who feels a great debt
body:
## "A liberal is someone who feels a great debt to his fellow man, which debt he proposes to pay off with your money."

G. Gordon Liddy

title: An unlimited power to tax
grift: An unlimited power to tax
body:
## "An unlimited power to tax involves, necessarily, a power to destroy."

Daniel Webster

title: Giving money and power to government
grift: Giving money and power to government
body:
## "Giving money and power to government is like giving whiskey and car keys to teenage boys."

P.J. O'Rourke

title: No government can exist without taxation
grift: No government can exist without taxation
body:
## "No government can exist without taxation. This money must necessarily be drawn from the pockets of the people."

John Marshall

title: The inherent vice of capitalism
grift: The inherent vice of capitalism
body:
## "The inherent vice of capitalism is the unequal sharing of blessings; the inherent virtue of socialism is the equal sharing of miseries."

Winston Churchill

title: The market is not an invention of capitalism
grift: The market is not an invention of capitalism
body:
## "The market is not an invention of capitalism. It has existed for centuries. It is an invention of civilization."

Mikhail Gorbachev

title: The power to tax involves the power to destroy
grift: The power to tax involves the power to destroy
body:
## "The power to tax involves the power to destroy."

John Marshall

title: You cannot multiply wealth
grift: You cannot multiply wealth
body:
## "You cannot multiply wealth by dividing it."

Adrian Rogers

title: A free people ought not only to be armed
grift: A free people ought not only to be armed
body:
## "A free people ought not only to be armed, but disciplined."

George Washington

title: A government resting on the minority is an aristocracy
grift: A government resting on the minority is an aristocracy
body:
## "A government resting on the minority is an aristocracy, not a democracy."

Thomas Jefferson

title: A people who mean to be their own governors
grift: A people who mean to be their own governors
body:
## "A people who mean to be their own governors must arm themselves with the power knowledge gives."

James Madison

title: A wise and frugal government
grift: A wise and frugal government
body:
## "A wise and frugal government shall restrain men from injuring one another, shall leave them otherwise free to regulate their own pursuits of industry and improvement."

Thomas Jefferson

title: All men having power ought to be distrusted
grift: All men having power ought to be distrusted
body:
## "All men having power ought to be distrusted to a certain degree."

James Madison

title: Democracy is two wolves and a lamb
grift: Democracy is two wolves and a lamb
body:
## "Democracy is two wolves and a lamb voting on what to have for lunch. Liberty is a well-armed lamb contesting the vote."

Benjamin Franklin (attributed)

title: Facts are stubborn things
grift: Facts are stubborn things
body:
## "Facts are stubborn things; and whatever may be our wishes, our inclinations, or the dictates of our passion, they cannot alter the state of facts and evidence."

John Adams

title: Give me liberty or give me death
grift: Give me liberty or give me death
body:
## "Give me liberty, or give me death!"

Patrick Henry

title: Government even in its best state
grift: Government even in its best state
body:
## "Government, even in its best state, is but a necessary evil; in its worst state, an intolerable one."

Thomas Paine

title: Government is best which governs the least
grift: Government is best which governs the least
body:
## "Government is best which governs the least, because its people discipline themselves."

Thomas Jefferson (attributed)

title: Government is not reason; it is not eloquent
grift: Government is not reason; it is not eloquent
body:
## "Government is not reason; it is not eloquent; it is force. Like fire, it is a dangerous servant and a fearful master."

George Washington

title: Guard against the impostures of pretended patriotism
grift: Guard against the impostures of pretended patriotism
body:
## "Guard against the impostures of pretended patriotism."

George Washington

title: He who dares not offend cannot be honest
grift: He who dares not offend cannot be honest
body:
## "He who dares not offend cannot be honest."

Thomas Paine

title: I cannot undertake to lay my finger
grift: I cannot undertake to lay my finger
body:
## "I cannot undertake to lay my finger on that article of the Constitution which granted a right to Congress of expending, on objects of benevolence, the money of their constituents."

James Madison

title: I own that I am not a friend to a very energetic government
grift: I own that I am not a friend to a very energetic government
body:
## "I own that I am not a friend to a very energetic government. It is always oppressive."

Thomas Jefferson

title: I predict future happiness for Americans
grift: I predict future happiness for Americans
body:
## "I predict future happiness for Americans, if they can prevent the government from wasting the labors of the people under the pretense of taking care of them."

Thomas Jefferson

title: I would rather be exposed to the inconveniences
grift: I would rather be exposed to the inconveniences
body:
## "I would rather be exposed to the inconveniences attending too much liberty than those attending too small a degree of it."

Thomas Jefferson

title: If men were angels
grift: If men were angels
body:
## "If men were angels, no government would be necessary."

James Madison

title: If you want to help the poor
grift: If you want to help the poor
body:
## "If you want to help the poor, demonstrate to them how they can help themselves."

Benjamin Franklin

title: It is the duty of the patriot
grift: It is the duty of the patriot
body:
## "It is the duty of the patriot to protect his country from its government."

Thomas Paine

title: Knowledge will forever govern ignorance
grift: Knowledge will forever govern ignorance
body:
## "Knowledge will forever govern ignorance; and a people who mean to be their own governors must arm themselves with the power which knowledge gives."

James Madison

title: Laws that forbid the carrying of arms
grift: Laws that forbid the carrying of arms
body:
## "Laws that forbid the carrying of arms disarm only those who are neither inclined nor determined to commit crimes."

Thomas Jefferson (quoting Cesare Beccaria)

title: Liberty must at all hazards be supported
grift: Liberty must at all hazards be supported
body:
## "Liberty must at all hazards be supported. We have a right to it, derived from our Maker."

John Adams

title: No freeman shall be debarred the use of arms
grift: No freeman shall be debarred the use of arms
body:
## "No freeman shall be debarred the use of arms."

Thomas Jefferson

title: Of all the enemies to public liberty
grift: Of all the enemies to public liberty
body:
## "Of all the enemies to public liberty, war is, perhaps, the most to be dreaded."

James Madison

title: Only a virtuous people are capable of freedom
grift: Only a virtuous people are capable of freedom
body:
## "Only a virtuous people are capable of freedom. As nations become corrupt and vicious, they have more need of masters."

Benjamin Franklin

title: Our Constitution was made only for a moral and religious people
grift: Our Constitution was made only for a moral and religious people
body:
## "Our Constitution was made only for a moral and religious people. It is wholly inadequate to the government of any other."

John Adams

title: Peace, commerce, and honest friendship
grift: Peace, commerce, and honest friendship
body:
## "Peace, commerce, and honest friendship with all nations; entangling alliances with none."

Thomas Jefferson

title: People who are willing to trade freedom for security
grift: People who are willing to trade freedom for security
body:
## "People who are willing to trade freedom for security will end up with neither."

Benjamin Franklin

title: The advancement and diffusion of knowledge
grift: The advancement and diffusion of knowledge
body:
## "The advancement and diffusion of knowledge is the only guardian of true liberty."

James Madison

title: The Constitution is not a suicide pact
grift: The Constitution is not a suicide pact
body:
## "The Constitution of the United States is a law for rulers and people, equally in war and in peace, and covers with the shield of its protection all classes of men, at all times and under all circumstances."

David Davis

title: The Constitution is not an instrument for the government
grift: The Constitution is not an instrument for the government
body:
## "The Constitution is not an instrument for the government to restrain the people, it is an instrument for the people to restrain the government."

Patrick Henry

title: The Constitution only gives people the right to pursue happiness
grift: The Constitution only gives people the right to pursue happiness
body:
## "The Constitution only gives people the right to pursue happiness. You have to catch it yourself."

Benjamin Franklin

title: The Constitution shall never be construed
grift: The Constitution shall never be construed
body:
## "The Constitution shall never be construed to prevent the people of the United States who are peaceable citizens from keeping their own arms."

Samuel Adams

title: The danger in democracy is not tyranny by the minority
grift: The danger in democracy is not tyranny by the minority
body:
## "The danger in democracy is not tyranny by the minority but tyranny by the majority."

James Madison

title: The first duty of society is justice
grift: The first duty of society is justice
body:
## "The first duty of society is justice."

Alexander Hamilton

title: The great danger in republics
grift: The great danger in republics
body:
## "The great danger in republics is that the majority may not sufficiently respect the rights of the minority."

James Madison

title: The legitimate powers of government extend
grift: The legitimate powers of government extend
body:
## "The legitimate powers of government extend to such acts only as are injurious to others."

Thomas Jefferson

title: The liberties of a people never were
grift: The liberties of a people never were
body:
## "The liberties of a people never were, nor ever will be, secure when the transactions of their rulers may be concealed from them."

Patrick Henry

title: The man who reads nothing
grift: The man who reads nothing
body:
## "The man who reads nothing at all is better educated than the man who reads nothing but newspapers."

Thomas Jefferson

title: The man who trades freedom for security
grift: The man who trades freedom for security
body:
## "The man who trades freedom for security does not deserve nor will he ever receive either."

Benjamin Franklin

title: The natural progress of things is for liberty to yield
grift: The natural progress of things is for liberty to yield
body:
## "The natural progress of things is for liberty to yield and government to gain ground."

Thomas Jefferson

title: The people are the only sure reliance
grift: The people are the only sure reliance
body:
## "The people are the only sure reliance for the preservation of our liberty."

Thomas Jefferson

title: The power under the Constitution will always be in the people
grift: The power under the Constitution will always be in the people
body:
## "The power under the Constitution will always be in the people."

George Washington

title: The price of freedom is eternal vigilance
grift: The price of freedom is eternal vigilance
body:
## "The price of freedom is eternal vigilance."

Thomas Jefferson

title: The purpose of a constitution
grift: The purpose of a constitution
body:
## "The purpose of a constitution is to restrain the government, not the people."

Patrick Henry

title: The rights of individuals should be the primary object
grift: The rights of individuals should be the primary object
body:
## "The rights of individuals should be the primary object of all governments."

Thomas Jefferson

title: The spirit of resistance to government
grift: The spirit of resistance to government
body:
## "The spirit of resistance to government is so valuable on certain occasions that I wish it to be always kept alive."

Thomas Jefferson

title: The tree of liberty must be refreshed
grift: The tree of liberty must be refreshed
body:
## "The tree of liberty must be refreshed from time to time with the blood of patriots and tyrants."

Thomas Jefferson

title: The ultimate authority resides in the people alone
grift: The ultimate authority resides in the people alone
body:
## "The ultimate authority resides in the people alone."

James Madison

title: Those who expect to reap the blessings of freedom
grift: Those who expect to reap the blessings of freedom
body:
## "Those who expect to reap the blessings of freedom must, like men, undergo the fatigue of supporting it."

Thomas Paine

title: Those who would give up essential liberty
grift: Those who would give up essential liberty
body:
## "Those who would give up essential liberty to purchase a little temporary safety deserve neither liberty nor safety."

Benjamin Franklin

title: To compel a man to furnish contributions
grift: To compel a man to furnish contributions
body:
## "To compel a man to furnish contributions of money for the propagation of opinions which he disbelieves and abhors is sinful and tyrannical."

Thomas Jefferson

title: To compel a man to furnish funds for the propagation of ideas he disbelieves
grift: To compel a man to furnish funds for the propagation of ideas he disbelieves
body:
## "To compel a man to furnish funds for the propagation of ideas he disbelieves and abhors is sinful and tyrannical."

Thomas Jefferson

title: To compel a man to subsidize
grift: To compel a man to subsidize
body:
## "To compel a man to subsidize the teaching of ideas he disbelieves and abhors is sinful and tyrannical."

Thomas Jefferson

title: Tyranny is defined as that which is legal for the government
grift: Tyranny is defined as that which is legal for the government
body:
## "Tyranny is defined as that which is legal for the government but illegal for the citizenry."

Thomas Jefferson

title: We are not to expect to be transported from despotism to liberty
grift: We are not to expect to be transported from despotism to liberty
body:
## "We are not to expect to be transported from despotism to liberty in a featherbed."

Thomas Jefferson

title: When injustice becomes law
grift: When injustice becomes law
body:
## "When injustice becomes law, resistance becomes duty."

Thomas Jefferson

title: When people fear the government, there is tyranny
grift: When people fear the government, there is tyranny
body:
## "When people fear the government, there is tyranny; when the government fears the people, there is liberty."

Thomas Jefferson

title: When the people fear the government
grift: When the people fear the government
body:
## "When the people fear the government, there is tyranny; when the government fears the people, there is liberty."

Thomas Jefferson

title: When the people find they can vote themselves money
grift: When the people find they can vote themselves money
body:
## "When the people find they can vote themselves money, that will herald the end of the republic."

Benjamin Franklin (attributed)

title: Without liberty, law loses its nature
grift: Without liberty, law loses its nature
body:
## "Without liberty, law loses its nature and justice loses its meaning."

Alexander Hamilton

title: The dark night of fascism is always descending
grift: The dark night of fascism is always descending
body:
## "The dark night of fascism is always descending in the United States and yet lands only in Europe."

Tom Wolfe

title: Socialism irons out the boom-bust cycle
grift: Socialism irons out the boom-bust cycle
body:
## "Socialism irons out the boom-bust cycle by eliminating the boom part."

Lawrence W. Reed

title: The state is that great fiction
grift: The state is that great fiction
body:
## "The state is that great fiction by which everyone tries to live at the expense of everyone else."

Frédéric Bastiat

title: When buying and selling are controlled by legislation
grift: When buying and selling are controlled by legislation
body:
## "When buying and selling are controlled by legislation, the first things to be bought and sold are legislators."

P.J. O'Rourke

title: A government big enough to give you everything you want
grift: A government big enough to give you everything you want
body:
## "A government big enough to give you everything you want is a government big enough to take from you everything you have."

Gerald R. Ford

title: A government that robs Peter
grift: A government that robs Peter
body:
## "A government that robs Peter to pay Paul can always depend on the support of Paul."

George Bernard Shaw

title: A man's admiration for absolute government
grift: A man's admiration for absolute government
body:
## "A man's admiration for absolute government is proportionate to the contempt he feels for those around him."

Alexis de Tocqueville

title: A state that dwarfs its men
grift: A state that dwarfs its men
body:
## "A state that dwarfs its men, in order that they may be more docile instruments in its hands even for beneficial purposes, will find that with small men no great thing can really be accomplished."

John Stuart Mill

title: Democracy must be something more
grift: Democracy must be something more
body:
## "Democracy must be something more than two wolves and a sheep voting on what to have for dinner."

James Bovard

title: Every step we take towards making the State our Caretaker
grift: Every step we take towards making the State our Caretaker
body:
## "Every step we take towards making the State our Caretaker of our lives, by that much we move toward making the State our Master."

Dwight D. Eisenhower

title: Everyone wants to live at the expense of the state
grift: Everyone wants to live at the expense of the state
body:
## "Everyone wants to live at the expense of the state. They forget that the state lives at the expense of everyone."

Frédéric Bastiat

title: Government has no other end
grift: Government has no other end
body:
## "Government has no other end, but the preservation of property."

John Locke

title: Government is the great fiction
grift: Government is the great fiction
body:
## "Government is the great fiction through which everybody endeavors to live at the expense of everybody else."

Frédéric Bastiat

title: If you have ten thousand regulations
grift: If you have ten thousand regulations
body:
## "If you have ten thousand regulations, you destroy all respect for the law."

Winston Churchill

title: In matters of conscience, the law of the majority has no place
grift: In matters of conscience, the law of the majority has no place
body:
## "In matters of conscience, the law of the majority has no place."

Mahatma Gandhi

title: It is not wisdom but Authority that makes a law
grift: It is not wisdom but Authority that makes a law
body:
## "It is not wisdom but Authority that makes a law."

Thomas Hobbes

title: Let us not seek the Republican answer or the Democratic answer

Vaclav Havel

Calvin Coolidge

title: The greatest dangers to liberty lurk in insidious encroachment

Louis D. Brandeis

title: Human beings are born with different capacities
grift: Human beings are born with different capacities
body:
## "Human beings are born with different capacities, if they are free, they are not equal. And it they are equal, they are not free."

Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn

title: Liberty is not a means to a higher political end
grift: Liberty is not a means to a higher political end

John Stuart Mill

title: The end of law is not to abolish or restrain

title: A free press stands as one of the great interpreters
grift: A free press stands as one of the great interpreters

Herbert Hoover

title: Civilization is the process of setting man free
grift: Civilization is the process of setting man free
body:
## "Civilization is the process of setting man free from men."

Ayn Rand

title: Eternal vigilance is the price of liberty
grift: Eternal vigilance is the price of liberty

## "Eternal vigilance is the price of liberty."

Wendell Phillips

Louis Brandeis

title: Freedom is never voluntarily given by the oppressor
grift: Freedom is never voluntarily given by the oppressor
body:

Martin Luther King Jr.

title: Liberty and independence forever
grift: Liberty and independence forever
body:
## "Liberty and independence forever."

Davey Crockett

title: Liberty is always dangerous
grift: Liberty is always dangerous
body:
## "Liberty is always dangerous, but it is the safest thing we have."

Harry Emerson Fosdick

title: Liberty is being able to engage
grift: Liberty is being able to engage
body:
## "Liberty is being able to engage in peaceable voluntary exchange without interference from others."

Walter E Williams

title: Liberty is not a means to a political end
grift: Liberty is not a means to a political end
body:
## "Liberty is not a means to a political end. It is itself the highest political end."

Lord Acton

Calvin Coolidge

title: Liberty is not the power of doing what we like

Lord Acton

title: No man's life, liberty or property is safe

body:
## "No man's life, liberty or property is safe while the legislature is in session."

Gideon J. Tucker (attributed to Mark Twain)

title: None are more hopelessly enslaved
grift: None are more hopelessly enslaved
body:
## "None are more hopelessly enslaved than those who falsely believe they are free."

Johann Wolfgang von Goethe

title: None can love freedom heartily
grift: None can love freedom heartily
body:
## "None can love freedom heartily but good men; the rest love not freedom but license."

John Milton

Alexis de Tocqueville

title: The history of liberty is a history of resistance
grift: The history of liberty is a history of resistance

## "The history of liberty is a history of resistance."

Woodrow Wilson

title: The issue is always the same

body:
## "The issue is always the same: the individual versus the collective."

Ayn Rand

Abraham Lincoln

title: The liberty of the individual must be thus far limited

John Stuart Mill

title: The natural liberty of man

body:
## "The natural liberty of man is to be free from any superior power on Earth."

Samuel Adams

title: The only freedom which deserves the name
grift: The only freedom which deserves the name
body:

John Stuart Mill

title: The only thing that saves us from bureaucracy

title: The progress of society depends not on legislation

title: The reason every Marxist government

It's that the ideology itself prioritizes class justice over individual rights and makes no allowance for legitimate disagreement."

Jonathan Chait

title: The right to be let alone
grift: The right to be let alone
body:
## "The right to be let alone is the most comprehensive of rights, and the right most valued by a free people."

Louis Brandeis

title: The smallest minority on earth is the individual

Alexis de Tocqueville

Fredrich Hayek

Paraphrasing JBS Haldane

Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn

grift: Young people are told at university
body:
## "Young people are told at university that they come from nowhere and belong to nothing: that all pre-existing forms of membership are null and void. They are offered a rite of passage into cultural nothingness, since this is the only way to achieve the egalitarian goal."

Roger Scruton

title: ~'A leftist is someone who feels a great debt'

G. Gordon Liddy

title: A 'liberal' is a man too broadminded
grift: A 'liberal' is a man too broadminded

## "A 'liberal' is a man too broadminded to take his own side in a quarrel."

Robert Frost

title: A man is no less a slave

Lysander Spooner

title: As Hayek himself understood
grift: As Hayek himself understood
body:
## "As Hayek himself understood, however, the case for freedom and free markets must continually be rejuvenated and made again and again and again. The project is never completed, as more recent political developments in Britain and the United States attest. Opposing ideas—those of collectivism of one form or another—are always being generated, refined, and spread. Failure by classical liberals and other defenders of a society based on free markets and strictly limited government to counter these collectivist ideas will guarantee the victory of collectivism."

Donald J. Boudreaux

title: Big Government is the small option
grift: Big Government is the small option
body:
## "Big Government is the small option: it's the guarantee of smaller freedom, smaller homes, smaller cars, smaller opportunities, smaller lives."

Mark Steyn

title: Chesterton's Fence: In the matter of reforming things
grift: Chesterton's Fence: In the matter of reforming things
body:
## Chesterton's Fence: "In the matter of reforming things, as distinct from deforming them, there is one plain and simple principle; a principle which will probably be called a paradox. There exists in such a case a certain institution or law; let us say, for the sake of simplicity, a fence or gate erected across a road. The more modern type of reformer goes gaily up to it and says, 'I don't see the use of this; let us clear it away.' To which the more intelligent type of reformer will do well to answer: 'If you don't see the use of it, I certainly won't let you clear it away. Go away and think. Then, when you can come back and tell me that you do see the use of it, I may allow you to destroy it.'"

G.K. Chesterton

title: Demography is Destiny
grift: Demography is Destiny
body:
## "Demography is destiny. When your birth rate collapses, you're not just shrinking your population—you're shrinking your culture, your values, your way of life."

Mark Steyn, 2012

title: Don't expect to build up the weak
grift: Don't expect to build up the weak
body:
## "Don't expect to build up the weak by pulling down the strong."

Calvin Coolidge

title: Don't fix what ain't broke.
grift: Don't fix what ain't broke.
body:
## "Don't fix what ain't broke."

Anonymous

body:

Frédéric Bastiat

title: Europe's problem is not that it has too many people
grift: Europe's problem is not that it has too many people
body:

Mark Steyn, America Alone

title: Every man has a property in his own person
grift: Every man has a property in his own person
body:
## "Every man has a property in his own person. This nobody has a right to, but himself."

John Locke

title: Everything that is really great
grift: Everything that is really great
body:
## "Everything that is really great and inspiring is created by the individual who can labour in freedom."

Albert Einstein

title: Extremism in the defense of liberty
grift: Extremism in the defense of liberty
body:
## "Extremism in the defense of liberty is no vice. And moderation in the pursuit of justice is no virtue.

Barry Goldwater

title: For Al Gore and Paul Ehrlich and Co
grift: For Al Gore and Paul Ehrlich and Co
body:
## "For Al Gore and Paul Ehrlich and Co., whatever the problem, the solution is always the same. Whether it's global cooling, global warming, or overpopulation, we need bigger government, more regulation, higher taxes, and a massive transfer of power from the citizens to some unelected self-perpetuating crisis lobby."

Mark Steyn

title: Freedom is messy. In free societies, people will fall
grift: Freedom is messy. In free societies, people will fall
body:
## "Freedom is messy. In free societies, people will fall through the cracks - drink too much, eat too much, buy unaffordable homes, fail to make prudent provision for health care, and much else. But the price of being relieved of all those tiresome choices by a benign paternal government is far too high."

Mark Steyn

title: Government does not solve problems
grift: Government does not solve problems
body:
## "Government does not solve problems; it subsidizes them."

Ronald Reagan

title: Government is a broker in pillage
grift: Government is a broker in pillage
body:
## "Government is a broker in pillage, and every election is a sort of advanced auction on stolen goods."

H L Mencken

title: He who opens a school door, closes a prison
grift: He who opens a school door, closes a prison
body:
## "He who opens a school door, closes a prison."

Victor Hugo

title: If all cultures are equal
grift: If all cultures are equal
body:
## "If all cultures are equal, then cannibalism is just a matter of culinary taste"

Leo Strauss

title: If I can't trust you not to put a boy
grift: If I can't trust you not to put a boy
body:
## "If I can't trust you not to put a boy in a teenage girl's locker room, how will I ever listen to your plan for taxes and the economy?... I will not, because l've already concluded you're a lunatic."

Scott Jennings

title: If liberty means anything
grift: If liberty means anything
body:
## "If liberty means anything at all, it means the right to tell people what they do not want to hear. If liberty means anything at all, it means the right to tell people what they do not want to hear them."

George Orwell

title: If the natural tendencies of mankind
grift: If the natural tendencies of mankind
body:
## "If the natural tendencies of mankind are so bad that it is not safe to permit people to be free, how is it that the tendencies of these organizers are always good? Do not the legislators and their appointed agents also belong to the human race? Or do they believe that they themselves are made of a finer clay than the rest of mankind?"

Frédéric Bastiat

title: If you're a descendant of a race of people
grift: If you're a descendant of a race of people
body:
## "If you're a descendant of a race of people who explored the known world, invented 95% of modern technology, civilized half the planet, and generated most of the philosophical ideas on which civilisation has been built, but you're ashamed of yourself, then you might be brainwashed."

Anon

title: If your schoolhouse is crumbling
grift: If your schoolhouse is crumbling
body:
## "If your schoolhouse is crumbling, your hospitals are overwhelmed, and your pension plans are bankrupt, it's not because of 'overpopulation' but because of underpopulation — too few people to sustain the systems we've built."

Mark Steyn

title: In politics, stupidity is not a handicap

## "In politics, stupidity is not a handicap."

Napoleon Bonaparte

title: Injustice anywhere is a threat to justice everywhere

body:
## "Injustice anywhere is a threat to justice everywhere."

Martin Luther King Jr.

title: It is difficult to free fools
grift: It is difficult to free fools
body:
## "It is difficult to free fools from the chains they revere."

Voltaire

title: It is with infinite caution that any man
grift: It is with infinite caution that any man
body:
## "It is with infinite caution that any man ought to venture upon pulling down an edifice which has answered in any tolerable degree for ages the common purposes of society, or on building it up again, without having models and patterns of approved utility before his eyes."

Edmund Burke

title: Jokes are one of the things that bind the culture
grift: Jokes are one of the things that bind the culture
body:
## "Jokes are one of the things that bind the culture. If you can't have jokes about everybody in society and if one group is hedged off and protected then that group can never truly be integrated."

grift: Let me offer you my definition of social justice
body:

Walter E Williams

Robert Novak

title: Never doubt that a small group of thoughtful citizens

Margaret Mead

title: Never let a crisis go to waste

## "Never let a crisis go to waste. They are opportunities to do big things."

Rahm Emanuel

title: No society can surely be flourishing

body:
## "No society can surely be flourishing and happy, of which the far greater part of the members are poor and miserable."

Adam Smith

title: None can love freedom heartily, but good men

title: Not all Muslims support terrorists

## "Not all Muslims support terrorists — though enough of them share their basic objectives (the wish to live under Islamic law in Europe and North America) to function wittingly or otherwise as the 'good cop' end of an Islamic good cop/bad cop routine."

Jonah Goldberg

title: Show me a young Conservative and
