title: So much of liberalism in its classical sense
grift: So much of liberalism in its classical sense
body:
## "So much of liberalism in its classical sense is taken for granted in the west today and even disrespected. We take freedom for granted, and because of this we don't understand how incredibly vulnerable it is."

Niall Ferguson

title: Teaching Latin and Greek in high school
grift: Teaching Latin and Greek in high school
body:
## "In 100 years we have gone from teaching Latin and Greek in high school to teaching Remedial English in college."

Joseph Sobran

title: The burden of proof lies on the one
grift: The burden of proof lies on the one
body:
## "The burden of proof lies on the one who wishes to change what has been tried and tested, not on the one who wishes to preserve it."

Roger Scruton

title: The danger is not that a particular class is unfit to govern
grift: The danger is not that a particular class is unfit to govern
body:
## "The danger is not that a particular class is unfit to govern. Every class is unfit to govern."

Lord Acton

title: The great aim of education is not knowledge but action
grift: The great aim of education is not knowledge but action
body:
## "The great aim of education is not knowledge but action."

Herbert Spencer

title: The hardest thing to explain is
grift: The hardest thing to explain is
body:
## "The hardest thing to explain is the glaringly evident which everybody has decided not to see."

Ayn Rand

title: The larger the government, the smaller the citizen
grift: The larger the government, the smaller the citizen
body:
## "The larger the government, the smaller the citizen."

Dennis Prager

title: The Left doesn't want to win the debate
grift: The Left doesn't want to win the debate
body:
## "The Left doesn't want to win the debate, they want to cancel the debate"

Mark Steyn

title: The liberties of a people
grift: The liberties of a people
body:
## "The liberties of a people will never be secure, when the transactions of their rulers may be concealed from them."

Patrick Henry

title: The man who is not allowed to own property
grift: The man who is not allowed to own property
body:
## "The man who is not allowed to own property is a man reduced to dependence."

John Locke

title: The moderate left are following the same
grift: The moderate left are following the same
body:
## "The moderate left are following the same character as the 'moderate Muslim': unlike their hardcore brethren they don't want to decapitate you themselves, but they do enjoy watching."

Mark Steyn

title: The most harm of all is done
grift: The most harm of all is done
body:
## "The most harm of all is done when power is in the hands of people who are absolutely persuaded of the purity of their intentions"

Milton Friedman

title: The politician attempts to remedy the evil
grift: The politician attempts to remedy the evil
body:
## "The politician attempts to remedy the evil by increasing the very thing that caused he evil in the first place: legal plunder."

Frédéric Bastiat

title: The problem isn't that Johnny can't read
grift: The problem isn't that Johnny can't read
body:
## "The problem isn't that Johnny can't read. The problem isn't even that Johnny can't think. The problem is that Johnny doesn't know what thinking is."

Richard Mitchell

title: The secret to happiness is freedom
grift: The secret to happiness is freedom
body:
## "The secret to happiness is freedom... And the secret to freedom is courage."

Thucydides

title: The ultimate result of shielding men from the effects of folly
grift: The ultimate result of shielding men from the effects of folly
body:
## "The ultimate result of shielding men from the effects of folly is to fill the world with fools."

Herbert Spencer

title: The urge to save humanity
grift: The urge to save humanity
body:
## "The urge to save humanity is almost always a false front for the urge to rule."

H.L. Mencken

title: The West's fertility rate
grift: The West's fertility rate
body:
## "The West's fertility rate is not just below replacement level; it's in a death spiral. Japan, Germany, Italy — they're not reproducing enough to maintain their populations, let alone their economies or cultures."

Mark Steyn,
From a 2010 interview with Hugh Hewitt.

title: There's no fine line between 'free speech' and 'hate speech
grift: There's no fine line between 'free speech' and 'hate speech
body:
## "There's no fine line between 'free speech' and 'hate speech': Free speech is hate speech; it's for the speech you hate—and for all your speech that the other guy hates. If you don't have free speech, then you can't have an honest discussion."

Mark Steyn

title: To educate a man in mind and not in morals
grift: To educate a man in mind and not in morals
body:
## "To educate a man in mind and not in morals is to educate a menace to society."

Theodore Roosevelt

title: To make men love their country, their country ought to be lovely
grift: To make men love their country, their country ought to be lovely
body:
## "To make men love their country, their country ought to be lovely."

Edmund Burke

title: To sin by silence when they should protest
grift: To sin by silence when they should protest
body:
## "To sin by silence when they should protest makes cowards of men."

Ella Wheeler Wilcox

title: Truth is treason in the empire of lies
grift: Truth is treason in the empire of lies
body:
## "Truth is treason in the empire of lies."

Ron Paul

title: Unlike classical liberalism, which saw the government as
grift: Unlike classical liberalism, which saw the government as
body:
## "Unlike classical liberalism, which saw the government as a necessary evil, or simply a benign but voluntary social contract for free men to enter into willingly, the belief that the entire society was one organic whole left no room for those who didn't want to behave, let alone 'evolve'".

Jonah Goldberg

title: We cannot doubt that self-interest is the mainspring
grift: We cannot doubt that self-interest is the mainspring
body:
## "We cannot doubt that self-interest is the mainspring of human nature. It must be clearly understood that this word is used here to designate a universal, incontestable fact, resulting from the nature of man, and not an adverse judgment, as would be the word selfishness."

Frédéric Bastiat

title: We must not regard every change as an improvement
grift: We must not regard every change as an improvement
body:
## "We must not regard every change as an improvement, nor must we think that what is ancient is necessarily good; but we should examine all things and retain what is good."

Aristotle

title: What is prudence in the conduct of every private family
grift: What is prudence in the conduct of every private family
body:
## "What is prudence in the conduct of every private family can scarce be folly in that of a great kingdom."

Adam Smith

title: What's worse than debauching your finances?
grift: What's worse than debauching your finances?
body:
## "What's worse than debauching your finances? Debauching your human capital..."

Mark Steyn

title: When all men are paid for existing
grift: When all men are paid for existing
body:
## "When all men are paid for existing and no man must pay for his sins, then the Party is over."

G.K. Chesterton

title: When goods don't cross borders, soldiers will
grift: When goods don't cross borders, soldiers will
body:
## "When goods don't cross borders, soldiers will."

Frédéric Bastiat

title: You cannot strengthen the weak by weakening the strong
grift: You cannot strengthen the weak by weakening the strong
body:
## "You cannot strengthen the weak by weakening the strong."

Abraham Lincoln

title: You can't fix a problem until you identify it
grift: You can't fix a problem until you identify it
body:
## "You can't fix a problem until you identify it honestly. Today, there are tens of millions of Americans who have absolutely no idea that we are broke—broker than any society has ever been. Granted, America is not alone in its appetites; the G7 is essentially a group of addicts run by pathological liars."

Mark Steyn
